title: ranunculus pollinensis
intro: Ranunculus pollinensis, also known as the lesser celandine, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Ranunculus pollinensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
